normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_feasibility_witness {intervals : List (Int × Int)} (hlt : ∀ iv ∈ intervals, iv.1 < iv.2) (k : Nat)
    (hk : k = interval_scheduling intervals) :
    ∃ S : List (Int × Int),
      (S ⊆ intervals) ∧
      (pairwise_nonoverlapping S) ∧
      (S.length = k.toNat)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned count is witnessed by some subset of the input that is pairwise non-overlapping.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_optimality {intervals : List (Int × Int)} (hlt : ∀ iv ∈ intervals, iv.1 < iv.2)
    (k : Nat) (hk : k = interval_scheduling intervals) :
    ∀ T : List (Int × Int),
      (T ⊆ intervals) →
      pairwise_nonoverlapping T →
      T.length ≤ k
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: No pairwise non-overlapping subset of the input is strictly larger than the returned count.
Empty input: the result is 0 when the input is empty.
Endpoint convention: intervals sharing only an endpoint are non-overlapping (using [start,end) so a.2 = b.1 is allowed).
